findingDetails.title: Empty upsert violates the documented no-op contract
findingDetails.evidence: spec.md documents empty upsert as bounded no-op, schema allows omitted nodes/edges, but handler returns error when both arrays are empty.
findingDetails.recommendation: Reconcile docs/schema/handler by either returning ok zero-count no-op or rejecting empty payloads at schema/docs level, then add a regression test.
findingDetails.scopeProof: councilGraphUpsertSchema optional nodes/edges feeds the only council_graph_upsert handler, which rejects the empty payload before persistence.

findingDetails.title: Verification claims convergence CONTINUE coverage that the council graph tests do not exercise
findingDetails.evidence: checklist.md claims STOP_ALLOWED, CONTINUE, and blocked critical disagreement coverage; reviewed tests assert STOP_BLOCKED and STOP_ALLOWED only while convergence.ts has a distinct CONTINUE branch.
findingDetails.recommendation: Add a convergence fixture that fails a non-blocking threshold and asserts decision === CONTINUE, then update checklist evidence.
findingDetails.scopeProof: The single council graph test file has three cases covering STOP_BLOCKED/STOP_ALLOWED/empty STOP_BLOCKED while CONTINUE remains unproven in the reviewed scope.

results.evidence: Empty-upsert handler rejects documented no-op contract.
CHK-022 claims CONTINUE convergence coverage; reviewed tests do not assert CONTINUE.
Schema/handler surfaces inspected; empty-upsert schema permissiveness conflicts with handler behavior.

graphEvents.label: Empty upsert violates documented no-op contract
Verification claims CONTINUE coverage not exercised

findingDetails.title: Arbitrary metadata is returned as prompt-safe output without redaction or size bounds
findingDetails.evidence: Schemas accept arbitrary node/edge metadata records, storage persists them verbatim, and prompt-safe serializers return full metadata objects through bounded query responses, violating the spec prompt-safe/no unrelated artifact exposure contract.
findingDetails.recommendation: Allowlist and size-bound response metadata or redact it by default, then add hostile/oversized metadata regression tests.
findingDetails.scopeProof: The same toPromptSafeNode/toPromptSafeEdge serializers feed unresolved_disagreements, evidence_chain, decision_support, convergence_blockers, and hot_nodes query outputs.

claimAdjudication.claim: Council graph query responses can expose arbitrary caller-supplied metadata despite the prompt-safe output contract.
claimAdjudication.counterevidenceSought: Checked schema validators, storage conversion, query serializers, query handlers, and council graph tests; found item-count/depth bounds and enum/path checks, but no metadata key allowlist, redaction, byte-size cap, or hostile-metadata regression test.
claimAdjudication.alternativeExplanation: Metadata is intended to carry confidence/severity signals for convergence, but the implementation accepts and returns any JSON object rather than just those safe fields.
claimAdjudication.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 only if a separate MCP-level redaction/response-size layer is proven to sanitize these handler payloads before model exposure.

traceabilityChecks.spec_code: fail: NFR-S02 requires prompt-safe outputs without unrelated packet artifact exposure, but metadata is accepted and returned without content redaction or size bounds.
traceabilityChecks.checklist_evidence: fail: CHK-031 claims input validation coverage, but reviewed tests do not cover hostile or oversized metadata response redaction.
traceabilityChecks.feature_catalog_code: partial: schema validation runs before council graph dispatch, but the schema permits arbitrary metadata.

edgeCases: Findings registry reported zero open findings while JSONL and strategy reported two active P1s; JSONL/strategy were treated as authoritative continuity.
BINDING lines were emitted after correcting an initial strategy-path typo; no target files were modified before artifact writes.
ruledOut: SQL injection through graph identifiers: prepared statements with bound parameters are used in reviewed database paths.
Cross-session reads through public handlers: handlers require sessionId and pass it into namespace-scoped queries.

findingDetails.title: Replay recovery remains manual despite documented rollback workflow
findingDetails.evidence: Recovery guidance tells operators to delete or ignore stale council-graph.sqlite rows and replay derived nodes/edges, while reviewed council graph DB and handlers expose only upsert/status/query/convergence paths and exact scoped searches found no delete/replay/reset implementation under lib/council-graph or handlers/council-graph.
findingDetails.recommendation: Add a bounded clear/rebuild helper or a documented replay command path that uses existing MCP surfaces, then cover stale-row recovery with a focused test.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Reviewed DB lifecycle, batchUpsert, handlers, tests, and graph_support.md recovery guidance; scoped exact searches for delete/replay/rollback/prune/clear/reset/remove under council graph lib and handlers returned no implementation hits.

noveltyJustification: One new P2 maintainability advisory was opened for rollback/replay ergonomics; no new P0/P1 was opened because source-of-truth artifacts remain authoritative and manual rebuild remains possible.
edgeCases: Findings registry remains stale relative to JSONL/prompt active findings; carry-forward uses state-log evidence until reducer refresh.
The first BINDING emission included a malformed target line before corrected canonical bindings; review scope stayed on the corrected resolved target.
Resource map is absent; resource-map coverage gate skipped.
ruledOut: No new P0/P1 maintainability issue was opened because the recovery gap is advisory while packet-local ai-council artifacts remain authoritative.
Did not retry exhausted SQL injection, cross-session read, or destructive source-of-truth concerns from prior iterations.
